title: fraxinus caroliniana var. oblaceolata
intro: Fraxinus caroliniana var. oblaceolata, also known as the Carolina ash or Coast white ash, is a variety of the Fraxinus caroliniana tree, native to the southeastern United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds, which have been found in its bark, leaves, and roots. The health benefits of Fraxinus caroliniana var. oblaceolata include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and support the immune system, as well as its use in treating various skin conditions and respiratory issues. Fraxinus caroliniana var. oblaceolata can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
